message.content:
@startuml title Toggle Switch participant "Ctc" as Ctc1 #90EE90# participant "TrackController\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \nCtc" as TrackController1 #90EE90# box "" #D3D3D3# participant "TrackModel\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \nTrackModel" as TrackModel2 #90EE90# participant "Block\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \nBlock" as Block4 #90EE90# end box rectangle "#FFFFFF#" { note right of TrackModel2 : getSwitch() -> setSwitchState("normal"\nor "alternate")\non switch object in block } == Select Block == activate Ctc1 deactivate Ctc1 ' There's a return arrow here from select block to CTC - not sure how this works? autonumber "[0]" setSwitchStatus(Block,direction) --> activate TrackController1 ref over TrackController1 , TrackModel2 ** Set direction on track model** setSwitchStatus(Block,direction)->> +TrackModel2 getblock()->>+Block4 note left of Block4 **Get the correct switch for our current location.**\nThe active controller will always be associated with one particular block at any given time.\nWe can use that information along with data about which way we want things pointed,\nto figure out what state should actually go into effect next... new_switch_status <-[dotted] deactivate TrackModel2 update_Switch_Status(block,status)<-[hidden]-+trackcontroller1 Note Right Of trackmodel,bottomright:"Normal"  <---> << I >Alternate >\\
These represent two different states where trains could potentially travel through depending upon their destination requirements etcetera..." @enduml
